correct_explanation: a near-maximum shared-memory allocation collapses active blocks per SM and prevents enough warps from running to hide latency
raw_response:
BOTTLENECK: occupancy-limited
REASONING: The achieved occupancy is only 8.33%, which is extremely low and directly caused by the massive dynamic shared memory allocation of 96 KB (24576 floats × 4 bytes) per block. On the A10G (GA102, 100 KB shared memory per SM), this limits execution to essentially one block per SM, and with only 128 threads per block, occupancy collapses. Despite the kernel being compute-heavy (arithmetic intensity of 577 FLOP/byte, well above the ridge point), the GPU cannot hide instruction latency with so few active warps, leaving compute units underutilized.
